title: practical english usage - Part 53
content:

It was the fifth time she had asked the same question.

It was the first time that I had heard her sing.

The  past perfect can be used to express an unrealised hope, wish, etc. Had is 2 unrealised        hopes     and   wishes;     things     that    did   not   happen When I wrote to her, she came at once.

had opened the window,...: one action caused the other.) When I opened the window, the cat jumped out. (More natural than When I from the second.) Lopened the windows, I sat down . ..: the first action was quite separate not - When I had opened the windows, I sat down and had a cup of tea. (     When Compare: meanings, so we  often have to show the exact time relations by the verb form.) This use of the past perfect is especially common with when. She didn't feel the same after her dog had died.

that we  are mainly talking about, but simply moving forward from one event to is not necessary in these cases, because we  are not 'going back' from the time two actions or events that happened one after the other.
